headline: Consumer Spending Slips, But These Niche Retail ETFs Are Defying The Downtrend
summary: Retail sales fell in May, but ETFs like ONLN, RTH, and IEDI gained as consumers spent on e-commerce, apparel, and discount retail.

headline: Apple, Google and Facebook Among Services Exposed In Massive Leak of More Than 16 Billion Login Records
summary: More than 16 billion login credentials from major platforms have been exposed, highlighting the widespread and organized nature of infostealer attacks.

summary: Daniel Ives' new AI ETF holds securities beyond the Magnificent 7 stocks, as he believes in looking past valuations for investments in tech.

headline: Amazon Retrains Warehouse Workers For Satellite Jobs: 'It Really Is Life-Changing,' Says Project Kuiper Technician
summary: Amazon.com Inc. is transforming warehouse workers into satellite technicians through education partnerships.

headline: Elon Musk's xAI May Face 'Clean Air Act' Lawsuit Over Alleged Pollution At Memphis Data Center
summary: The data center operated by Elon Musk's xAI in Memphis is facing potential legal action for running a fleet of natural gas turbines without the necessary permits.

headline: Bill Gates Lands Nvidia As Partner In $650 Million Nuclear Push To Power AI Revolution
summary: Nvidia has joined a $650 million investment in Bill Gates–backed TerraPower to support the development of next-gen nuclear reactors that could power energy-hungry AI data centers by 2030.

headline: YouTube Shorts Will Soon Feature Veo 3 AI Video Tools With Audio And Higher Quality — But Creators May Need A Paid Plan
summary: YouTube will integrate Google's Veo 3 AI video model into Shorts later this summer, offering higher-quality visuals and audio features, though access may require a paid subscription.

headline: Amazon-Backed Zoox Launches California Robotaxi Plant: Tesla Rival Targets 10,000 Vehicles A Year
summary: Amazon's robotaxi company Zoox announces new production facility in California as self-driving taxi sector grows.

headline: Palantir's Head Of Defense Says 'Xi Jinping Is Decoupling From Us' — Warns America Risks Losing Leverage If China Tech Shift Goes Unchecked
summary: Mike Gallagher, Head of Defense at Palantir Technologies Inc., warned that Chinese President Xi Jinping is actively decoupling from the United States and that America risks losing negotiating leverage by ignoring this strategic shift.

headline: Texas Lawmakers Urge Elon Musk's Tesla To Delay June 22 Robotaxi Launch Till September: Report
summary: A group of Texas lawmakers urge Tesla to delay Robotaxi launch in Austin on June 22 due to updated regulations taking effect in September.

headline: Are Markets Open On Juneteenth This Year? Here's What's Open And What's Closed On June 19
summary: The U.S. markets will be closed on Thursday, June 19, in observance of Juneteenth National Independence Day, a federal holiday commemorating the end of slavery in the United States.

headline: Mark Zuckerberg's Meta Reportedly Looking To Hire Former GitHub CEO Nat Friedman And Investor Daniel Gross As Part Of Aggressive AI Expansion Strategy
summary: Meta is reportedly in talks to hire former GitHub CEO Nat Friedman and investor Daniel Gross, and may partially acquire their AI fund NFDG, as part of a broader push to strengthen its AI capabilities and compete for top talent.

headline: Microsoft Study Finds That 9-To-5 Has Morphed Into 'Infinite Workdays' And Employees Can't Stop Checking Mail Even At Midnight
summary: Microsoft warns that the traditional 9-to-5 workday has dissolved into an infinite workday, with employees constantly checking email and joining impromptu meetings, potentially hindering productivity.

headline: Apple Hardware Executive Says iPhone Maker Plans To Use AI For Speeding Up Custom Chip Design: 'High Potential In Getting More Design Work In Less Time'
summary: Apple's top hardware executive Johny Srouji said the company plans to use generative AI to accelerate custom chip design, calling it a major productivity boost and highlighting Apple's bold, no-backup transition to Apple Silicon.
